req.title: Transaction dispute (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall dispute a transaction within 60 days of posting; disputes beyond 60 days shall be rejected. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

doc.reasoning: R-FIN-059 defines a 60-day eligibility window for transaction disputes. The critical partition boundary is day 60 (inclusive = eligible, exclusive = rejected). Four test cases are generated: (1) EP positive — a transaction clearly within the window (day 30) to confirm happy-path creation and provisional credit; (2) NEG — a transaction clearly beyond the window (day 90) to confirm 422 rejection; (3) BVA boundary-in — a transaction exactly at day 60 (the last valid day) to confirm acceptance at the upper boundary; (4) BVA boundary-out — a transaction at day 61 (first invalid day) to confirm rejection. Additional negative-path variants cover insufficient-funds and unauthorised scenarios as required by the statement. Together, these cases achieve EP, BVA, NEG, STC, and DT coverage of the dispute lifecycle state transitions.

tests.name: test_dispute_within_window_creates_provisional_credit
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Transaction exists posted exactly 30 days ago (well within 60-day window)
Transaction is in SETTLED state and has not been previously disputed
actions: POST /disputes with transaction_id referencing a 30-day-old transaction
Include valid dispute reason and customer_id in request body
expected: Response body contains provisional_credit.amount matching transaction amount
trace: R-FIN-059: provisional credit applied on creation
EP: representative value from valid partition [0, 60] days
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid
from datetime import datetime, timezone, timedelta

def test_dispute_within_window_creates_provisional_credit(api_client, create_transaction):
    txn = create_transaction(age_days=30, amount=150.00, state="SETTLED")
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "customer_id": txn["customer_id"],
        "reason": "UNRECOGNISED_CHARGE",
        "idempotency_key": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["dispute_id"] is not None
    assert body["status"] == "PENDING"
    credit = body["provisional_credit"]
    assert credit["applied"] is True
    assert float(credit["amount"]) == pytest.approx(150.00, rel=1e-2)

tests.name: test_dispute_beyond_60_days_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Transaction exists posted exactly 90 days ago (clearly outside 60-day window)
Transaction is in SETTLED state and has not been previously disputed
actions: POST /disputes with transaction_id referencing a 90-day-old transaction
Include valid dispute reason and customer_id in request body
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_dispute_beyond_60_days_returns_422(api_client, create_transaction):
    txn = create_transaction(age_days=90, amount=200.00, state="SETTLED")
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "customer_id": txn["customer_id"],
        "reason": "UNRECOGNISED_CHARGE",
        "idempotency_key": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "DISPUTE_WINDOW_EXPIRED"
    assert "dispute_id" not in body

tests.name: test_dispute_at_exactly_60_days_is_accepted
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Transaction exists posted exactly 60 days ago (last valid day, upper boundary)
Transaction is in SETTLED state and has not been previously disputed
actions: POST /disputes with transaction_id referencing a transaction posted exactly 60 days ago
Include valid dispute reason and customer_id in request body
trace: R-FIN-059: boundary — day 60 is the last permissible day (inclusive)
BVA: upper boundary of valid partition [0, 60]
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_dispute_at_exactly_60_days_is_accepted(api_client, create_transaction):
    txn = create_transaction(age_days=60, amount=75.50, state="SETTLED")
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "customer_id": txn["customer_id"],
        "reason": "DUPLICATE_CHARGE",
        "idempotency_key": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["dispute_id"] is not None
    assert body["status"] == "PENDING"
    assert body["provisional_credit"]["applied"] is True

tests.name: test_dispute_at_day_61_is_rejected
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Transaction exists posted exactly 61 days ago (first invalid day, just over boundary)
Transaction is in SETTLED state and has not been previously disputed
actions: POST /disputes with transaction_id referencing a transaction posted exactly 61 days ago
Include valid dispute reason and customer_id in request body
trace: R-FIN-059: boundary — day 61 is the first day outside the window
BVA: lower boundary of invalid partition (61, infinity)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_dispute_at_day_61_is_rejected(api_client, create_transaction):
    txn = create_transaction(age_days=61, amount=99.99, state="SETTLED")
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "customer_id": txn["customer_id"],
        "reason": "DUPLICATE_CHARGE",
        "idempotency_key": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "DISPUTE_WINDOW_EXPIRED"
    assert "dispute_id" not in body

tests.name: test_dispute_on_insufficient_funds_transaction_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Transaction exists posted 10 days ago but carries status DECLINED_INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS
Declined transaction is within 60-day window but is ineligible by business rule
actions: POST /disputes with transaction_id referencing an INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS declined transaction
Include valid dispute reason and customer_id in request body
trace: R-FIN-059: negative paths — insufficient funds must be tested
DT: decision rule — transaction state DECLINED_INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS => not disputable
NEG: error pathway for ineligible transaction state
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_dispute_on_insufficient_funds_transaction_returns_422(api_client, create_transaction):
    txn = create_transaction(
        age_days=10,
        amount=500.00,
        state="DECLINED_INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"
    )
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "customer_id": txn["customer_id"],
        "reason": "UNRECOGNISED_CHARGE",
        "idempotency_key": str(uuid.uuid4()),
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["error"]["code"] == "TRANSACTION_NOT_DISPUTABLE"
    assert "dispute_id" not in body

tests.name: test_dispute_on_unauthorised_transaction_transitions_to_under_review
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with a valid bearer token
Transaction exists posted 5 days ago in SETTLED state, flagged as POTENTIALLY_UNAUTHORISED
Transaction is within the 60-day dispute window
actions: POST /disputes with transaction_id and reason UNAUTHORISED
expected: GET returns dispute with status transitioning to 'UNDER_REVIEW'
trace: R-FIN-059: negative path — unauthorised transactions must be tested
STC: SETTLED -> DISPUTED(PENDING) -> UNDER_REVIEW state transition
R-FIN-059: dispute created; provisional credit applied
tests.executable:
import pytest
import uuid

def test_dispute_on_unauthorised_transaction_transitions_to_under_review(
    api_client, create_transaction, poll_until
):
    txn = create_transaction(
        age_days=5,
        amount=320.00,
        state="SETTLED",
        flags=["POTENTIALLY_UNAUTHORISED"]
    )
    idem_key = str(uuid.uuid4())
    payload = {
        "transaction_id": txn["id"],
        "customer_id": txn["customer_id"],
        "reason": "UNAUTHORISED",
        "idempotency_key": idem_key,
    }
    post_resp = api_client.post("/disputes", json=payload)
    assert post_resp.status_code == 201
    body = post_resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "PENDING"
    assert body["provisional_credit"]["applied"] is True
    dispute_id = body["dispute_id"]
    get_resp = poll_until(
        lambda: api_client.get(f"/disputes/{dispute_id}"),
        condition=lambda r: r.json()["status"] == "UNDER_REVIEW",
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert get_resp.json()["fraud_flag"] is True
